articles.headline: Police Raids in J&K Target Books Linked to Outlawed Jamaat-e-Islami, Raise Free Speech Concerns
articles.explainer:
• A series of police raids were conducted across bookstores in Jammu and Kashmir.
• 668 books published by an entity associated with the outlawed Islamic organisation Jamaat-e-Islami were seized.
• Authorities stated the action was taken to curb the spread of radical ideology and maintain public order.
• Civil rights groups and some political analysts have raised concerns about freedom of expression and academic liberty.
• The incident highlights ongoing tensions regarding security and fundamental rights in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights
freedom of speech and expression
security challenges and their management in border areas
role of state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: Jamaat-e-Islami is an outlawed organization in India.
Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
Reasonable restrictions on freedom of speech are outlined in Article 19(2).
Jammu and Kashmir's status post-Article 370 abrogation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the balance between national security and fundamental rights, specifically freedom of expression, in sensitive regions like J&K.
Analyze the implications of banning publications for intellectual discourse and the spread of ideologies.
Examine the legal framework (e.g., UAPA) used to justify such actions and its potential for misuse.
Suggest measures to counter radicalization while upholding constitutional liberties.
articles.imageDescription: Police officials inspecting seized books in a bookstore in Jammu and Kashmir.
articles.trivia: The first major crackdown on Jamaat-e-Islami in J&K occurred in the late 1980s, leading to its initial ban.

articles.headline: Tragic Collision Claims Ten Lives En Route to Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, Raises Pilgrim Safety Concerns
articles.explainer:
• An SUV carrying pilgrims collided with a bus in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh.
• All ten occupants of the SUV, who were en route to the 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, were killed.
• The accident occurred on a busy highway, prompting an immediate investigation into its causes.
• The incident has highlighted critical issues regarding road safety and pilgrim transportation during major religious gatherings.
• Authorities have announced ex-gratia payments and initiated measures to review safety protocols for the ongoing Mela.
gsSubTopics: social problems
disaster and disaster management
role of voluntary organizations
prelimsPoints: Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela is one of the largest religious gatherings globally.
Kumbh Mela is held every 12 years at four different locations: Prayagraj, Haridwar, Ujjain, and Nashik.
National Road Safety Policy aims to reduce road accident fatalities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges in ensuring road safety and crowd management during large-scale religious events like the Kumbh Mela.
Discuss the role of technology and inter-agency coordination in preventing such accidents.
Propose comprehensive strategies for pilgrim safety, including vehicle fitness, driver training, and emergency response.
Evaluate the economic and social impact of such accidents on affected communities and the tourism sector.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services at the site of the tragic vehicle collision in Prayagraj.
articles.trivia: The Kumbh Mela was inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2017.

articles.headline: Crowd Crush at New Delhi Railway Station Kills 18, Prompts Urgent Review of Public Safety Protocols
articles.explainer:
• At least 18 people were killed in a crowd crush incident at New Delhi railway station.
• The incident occurred during peak hours, reportedly due to overcrowding on a specific platform and inadequate crowd control measures.
• Emergency services were swiftly deployed, but the high density of people made rescue operations challenging.
• The tragedy has led to a major public outcry and calls for a thorough investigation into railway station management and disaster preparedness.
• The Ministry of Railways has ordered a high-level inquiry and announced compensation for the victims' families.
gsSubTopics: urbanization
their problems and their remedies
disaster and disaster management
public infrastructure
prelimsPoints: New Delhi Railway Station is one of India's busiest railway hubs.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) provides guidelines for crowd management.
Indian Railways operates under the Ministry of Railways.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to crowd crush incidents in public spaces, particularly in densely populated urban centers.
Evaluate the existing public safety and disaster management protocols in Indian railway stations and suggest improvements.
Analyze the role of urban planning, infrastructure development, and technology in preventing such tragedies.
Examine the accountability mechanisms for public authorities in ensuring the safety of commuters.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency personnel attending to victims at the New Delhi railway station after a crowd crush.
articles.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world, carrying over 8 billion passengers annually.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Volatile Inflation, Signals Cautious Optimism
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest review meeting.
• The MPC decided to maintain the repo rate and reverse repo rate at existing levels, opting for a status quo.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of persistently volatile retail inflation and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted concerns over food inflation but expressed cautious optimism regarding future growth prospects.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with potential for future adjustments based on evolving economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
inflation
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain a status quo on policy rates.
Discuss the challenges posed by food inflation and global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy framework.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in achieving both price stability and economic growth.
Examine the potential impact of the RBI's stance on various sectors of the economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The first meeting of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was held in October 2016.

articles.headline: India and UAE Hold High-Level Review of CEPA, Eyeing Enhanced Trade and Investment
articles.explainer:
• India and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) held a joint committee meeting to review the progress of their Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA).
• The review focused on identifying new avenues for cooperation and addressing implementation challenges.
• Both nations expressed satisfaction with the significant boost in bilateral trade and investment since CEPA's implementation.
• Discussions included expanding cooperation in green energy, digital trade, and logistics.
• The CEPA aims to increase bilateral non-oil trade to $100 billion by 2030.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: CEPA between India and UAE came into effect in May 2022.
UAE is India's third-largest trading partner and second-largest export destination.
CEPA covers trade in goods, services, rules of origin, customs procedures, and digital trade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-UAE CEPA for both nations.
Discuss the impact of CEPA on India's trade balance, export diversification, and energy security.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in expanding bilateral cooperation beyond traditional trade areas, such as green energy and digital economy.
Examine how such bilateral agreements contribute to India's broader foreign policy objectives and economic diplomacy.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and UAE flags displayed during a high-level bilateral meeting.
articles.trivia: UAE is home to over 3.5 million Indian expatriates, forming the largest expatriate community in the country.

articles.headline: PM-GatiShakti's Second Phase Focuses on Integrated Urban Infrastructure Development
articles.explainer:
• The government announced the initiation of the second phase of the PM-GatiShakti National Master Plan, specifically targeting urban infrastructure.
• This phase aims to integrate planning and execution of projects like metro extensions, smart city initiatives, and urban logistics hubs.
• The initiative leverages geospatial technology and data analytics for efficient resource allocation and project monitoring.
• The goal is to enhance urban connectivity, reduce logistics costs, and improve the quality of life in Indian cities.
• Stakeholders from various ministries and state governments are being brought together for coordinated implementation.
gsSubTopics: infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
investment models
planning
mobilization of resources
prelimsPoints: PM-GatiShakti was launched in October 2021.
It is a digital platform to bring 16 ministries together for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
The core principle is 'holistic planning' rather than 'siloed planning'.
It uses a GIS-based platform for visualization and planning.
mainsPoints: Explain how the PM-GatiShakti framework can revolutionize urban infrastructure development in India.
Discuss the benefits of integrated planning and execution of projects for urban logistics and economic efficiency.
Analyze the challenges in implementing such large-scale multi-sectoral projects, particularly concerning inter-ministerial coordination and land acquisition.
Suggest measures to ensure inclusive and sustainable urban development under PM-GatiShakti.
articles.imageDescription: A digital map showing integrated urban infrastructure projects under PM-GatiShakti.
articles.trivia: The PM-GatiShakti portal integrates satellite imagery and real-time data to track project progress.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Aadhaar Linkage for Select Welfare Schemes, Stresses Data Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment regarding the mandatory linkage of Aadhaar for certain government welfare schemes.
• The court largely upheld the constitutional validity of Aadhaar linkage for schemes that involve direct benefit transfers, citing prevention of leakages.
• However, it emphasized the need for robust data protection mechanisms and strict adherence to privacy principles.
• The verdict provides clarity on the balance between state's welfare objectives and citizens' right to privacy.
• The judgment also called for a review of the existing Aadhaar Act to strengthen data security provisions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
right to privacy
prelimsPoints: The Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act was passed in 2016.
The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right under Article 21.
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) scheme aims to transfer subsidies directly to beneficiaries' bank accounts.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's rationale in balancing the state's interest in efficient welfare delivery with the individual's right to privacy concerning Aadhaar.
Discuss the implications of this judgment for the implementation of government welfare schemes and the future of India's digital public infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in ensuring data security and preventing misuse of Aadhaar data, particularly in the absence of a comprehensive data protection law.
Propose legislative and technological measures to strengthen privacy safeguards in the context of digital identity systems.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The Aadhaar project was initially conceived in 2009 under the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI).

articles.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Mars Orbiter Mission-2 (MOM-2) Development, Aims for Advanced Martian Science
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) released an update on the progress of its Mars Orbiter Mission-2 (MOM-2), also known as Mangalyaan-2.
• Scientists confirmed that significant advancements have been made in payload development and mission architecture.
• MOM-2 aims for a more ambitious scientific exploration of Mars, focusing on surface features, atmospheric dynamics, and potential biosignatures.
• The mission will incorporate advanced instruments and potentially a lander or rover component for in-situ studies.
• The launch is tentatively scheduled for the late 2020s, building upon the success of the first Mars Orbiter Mission.
gsSubTopics: achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
space technology
prelimsPoints: India's first Mars Orbiter Mission (MOM) was launched in 2013 and successfully inserted into Mars orbit in 2014.
MOM made India the first Asian nation and ISRO the fourth space agency to reach Mars orbit.
MOM-2 is envisioned as a follow-up mission with more advanced scientific objectives.
PSLV and GSLV are ISRO's primary launch vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's Mars exploration program for its scientific capabilities and global standing in space technology.
Analyze the scientific objectives of MOM-2 and how they differ from or build upon MOM-1.
Examine the technological challenges involved in interplanetary missions, particularly for a lander/rover component.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits and spin-off technologies derived from space exploration missions for India.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Mars Orbiter Mission-2 spacecraft orbiting Mars.
articles.trivia: MOM-1 was the cheapest Mars mission to date, costing approximately $74 million.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Gathers Momentum with New Industry Investments and Policy Incentives
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is showing significant progress, attracting substantial investments from both domestic and international players.
• The government announced new policy incentives and subsidies to accelerate the development of green hydrogen production and infrastructure.
• Several major industrial conglomerates have unveiled plans for large-scale green hydrogen projects, including electrolyzer manufacturing.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Challenges remain in scaling up production, reducing costs, and developing a robust supply chain.
gsSubTopics: conservation
environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
science and technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission was approved by the Union Cabinet in January 2023.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
It aims to reduce reliance on fossil fuels and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
Electrolyzers are key equipment for green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Explain the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security, climate goals, and economic growth.
Discuss the key pillars and policy incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Suggest measures to foster a robust ecosystem for research, manufacturing, and deployment of green hydrogen technologies.
articles.imageDescription: An industrial facility depicting green hydrogen production via electrolysis powered by wind turbines.
articles.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Global Food Security at G20 Ministerial Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India played a prominent role at the G20 Agriculture Ministers' Meeting, advocating for a more equitable and resilient global food security system.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for sustainable agricultural practices, diversification of food sources, and reduction of food waste.
• Discussions included addressing the impact of climate change on agriculture and ensuring fair access to food for vulnerable populations.
• India highlighted its own initiatives, such as the National Food Security Act and promotion of millets, as models for other nations.
• The meeting concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing cooperation to tackle global hunger and malnutrition.
gsSubTopics: important international institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
issues relating to poverty and hunger
effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The National Food Security Act (NFSA) 2013 aims to provide subsidized food grains to approximately two-thirds of India's population.
The year 2023 was declared as the International Year of Millets by the UN, at India's initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and contributions to global food security initiatives within multilateral forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges to achieving global food security, including climate change, geopolitical conflicts, and supply chain disruptions.
Evaluate India's domestic policies and programs aimed at ensuring food security and their potential for replication elsewhere.
Propose strategies for international cooperation to build resilient and sustainable food systems globally.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from G20 nations attending a ministerial meeting on agriculture and food security.
articles.trivia: India holds 11.5% of the world's total livestock population, making it a significant player in global food production.

articles.headline: India Unveils Draft Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence Governance, Emphasizing Ethical Use
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a draft policy framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance in India.
• The framework focuses on promoting responsible AI development, ensuring ethical deployment, and mitigating potential risks.
• Key areas addressed include data privacy, algorithmic bias, accountability, and the socio-economic impact of AI.
• The government aims to foster innovation while establishing guardrails to prevent misuse and ensure public trust in AI technologies.
• Public consultations have been invited to gather feedback from stakeholders across industry, academia, and civil society.
gsSubTopics: science and technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
IT, Computers, Robotics, Nanotechnology, Bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
cyber security
prelimsPoints: MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT, electronics, and internet policy in India.
AI is a branch of computer science focused on creating intelligent machines.
Algorithmic bias refers to systematic and repeatable errors in a computer system due to flawed assumptions in the machine learning process.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of a robust AI governance framework for India, considering both opportunities and challenges posed by AI.
Analyze the key principles and provisions of the proposed draft policy, particularly concerning ethical AI, data privacy, and accountability.
Examine the potential socio-economic impacts of widespread AI adoption in India, including job displacement and skill requirements.
Suggest measures to balance innovation in AI with the need for regulation and public trust.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustration depicting AI ethics and governance with interconnected nodes.
articles.trivia: India is a founding member of the Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence (GPAI), an international initiative for responsible AI development.

articles.headline: Public Consultations Continue on Uniform Civil Code, Highlighting Diverse Views and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• Nationwide public consultations on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) are ongoing, with various religious and civil society groups submitting their views.
• The Law Commission of India is actively engaging with stakeholders to understand the complexities and implications of a UCC.
• Discussions reveal a wide spectrum of opinions, ranging from strong support for gender justice and national integration to concerns about religious freedom and cultural diversity.
• The process aims to build consensus and address apprehensions regarding the potential impact on personal laws of different communities.
• The government reiterates its commitment to a comprehensive and inclusive approach before any legislative proposal.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution states that the State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
Personal laws in India are currently governed by religious scriptures and customs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and historical background of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, national integration, and religious freedom.
Examine the challenges in achieving consensus on UCC, given India's diverse cultural and religious landscape.
Propose a roadmap for a phased and inclusive approach to UCC implementation, addressing concerns of various communities.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image depicting diverse religious symbols converging, representing the Uniform Civil Code debate.
articles.trivia: The first Law Commission in independent India was established in 1955.

articles.headline: New Conservation Sanctuary Declared in Western Ghats to Protect Endangered Species
articles.explainer:
• A new wildlife conservation sanctuary has been officially declared in a critical biodiversity hotspot within the Western Ghats region.
• The sanctuary aims to provide enhanced protection for several endangered and endemic species, including the Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque.
• The declaration involves significant efforts in habitat restoration, anti-poaching measures, and community engagement for sustainable conservation.
• This move is part of India's broader commitment to preserving its rich biodiversity and achieving international conservation targets.
• Environmentalists hail the decision as a crucial step towards safeguarding one of the world's most ecologically sensitive regions.
gsSubTopics: conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
biodiversity
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Species like Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque are endemic to the Western Ghats.
The Wildlife Protection Act, 1972, provides for the creation of wildlife sanctuaries and national parks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from developmental activities and climate change.
Analyze the importance of declaring new protected areas for biodiversity conservation in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing conservation strategies, including human-wildlife conflict and sustainable livelihood for local communities.
Suggest a multi-faceted approach involving policy, community participation, and scientific research for effective conservation of endangered species.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a depiction of local wildlife.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and influence India's monsoon weather patterns.

articles.headline: Defence Acquisition Council Clears Indigenous Submarine Construction Project, Boosts Naval Strength
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, approved a major indigenous submarine construction project.
• The 'Project P-75 (I)' initiative aims to build advanced conventional submarines with Air Independent Propulsion (AIP) technology in India.
• This approval signifies a significant leap towards 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) in defence manufacturing.
• The project will enhance the Indian Navy's underwater warfare capabilities and reduce reliance on foreign suppliers.
• It is expected to generate significant employment and boost the domestic defence industrial base.
gsSubTopics: security challenges and their management in border areas
various security forces and agencies and their mandate
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
defence technology
prelimsPoints: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body of the Ministry of Defence for capital acquisitions.
Project P-75 (I) is a follow-on project to Project 75 (Scorpene class submarines).
Air Independent Propulsion (AIP) technology allows non-nuclear submarines to operate without surfacing for longer periods.
Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan is a vision to make India a self-reliant nation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic necessity of modernizing the Indian Navy's submarine fleet, particularly in the context of evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
Analyze how indigenous submarine construction projects align with the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative and its implications for India's defence industrial base.
Examine the technological challenges and opportunities associated with developing and integrating advanced technologies like AIP in submarines.
Evaluate the economic benefits, including job creation and skill development, from such large-scale defence projects.
articles.imageDescription: A blueprint of an advanced conventional submarine with AIP technology.
articles.trivia: India currently operates both conventional (diesel-electric) and nuclear-powered submarines.

articles.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Providing Tap Water to Rural Households
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), highlighting significant achievements in rural tap water connectivity.
• The mission has successfully provided functional household tap connections (FHTC) to millions of additional rural households across the country.
• Several states and Union Territories are nearing 100% saturation, demonstrating effective implementation and community participation.
• The focus remains on ensuring long-term sustainability of water sources and water quality monitoring.
• JJM aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024.
gsSubTopics: government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission was launched in August 2019.
It comes under the Ministry of Jal Shakti.
The mission aims to provide FHTC to every rural household by 2024.
Pani Samitis (Village Water and Sanitation Committees) play a crucial role in local implementation.
mainsPoints: Assess the impact of the Jal Jeevan Mission on rural livelihoods, health, and women's empowerment.
Discuss the key strategies and challenges in achieving 100% tap water connectivity in diverse geographical regions of India.
Examine the role of community participation and decentralized governance (Pani Samitis) in the success of JJM.
Propose measures to ensure the long-term sustainability of water sources and address issues of water quality and equitable distribution.
articles.imageDescription: A rural woman collecting water from a newly installed tap connection.
articles.trivia: The 'Har Ghar Jal' (Tap water to every home) initiative is a major component of the Jal Jeevan Mission.

articles.headline: Indian Tourism Sector Shows Robust Revival, But Faces New Challenges in Sustainability and Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Indian tourism sector has demonstrated a strong post-pandemic revival, with increasing domestic and international tourist arrivals.
• Government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and enhanced visa facilities have contributed to this growth.
• However, the sector faces challenges related to sustainable tourism practices, inadequate infrastructure in remote areas, and skilled workforce shortages.
• Discussions are underway to promote responsible tourism, heritage conservation, and eco-tourism to ensure long-term growth.
• The Ministry of Tourism is collaborating with states and private players to address these issues and enhance India's global tourism competitiveness.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India's tourism sector is a significant contributor to GDP and employment.
'Dekho Apna Desh' is an initiative to encourage citizens to visit domestic tourist destinations.
The Ministry of Tourism is the nodal agency for the development and promotion of tourism in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the tourism sector in India's economic growth, employment generation, and cultural preservation.
Discuss the key challenges hindering the sustainable growth of Indian tourism, including infrastructure gaps and environmental concerns.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and initiatives in promoting tourism and addressing its challenges.
Propose strategies for developing India as a leading global tourist destination while ensuring environmental sustainability and community benefits.
articles.imageDescription: Tourists exploring a historical monument in India, symbolizing the revival of the sector.
articles.trivia: The World Travel & Tourism Council (WTTC) ranked India 10th globally in terms of travel and tourism's total contribution to GDP in 2019.

articles.headline: India Deploys New Contingent to UN Peacekeeping Mission, Reaffirms Commitment to Global Peace
articles.explainer:
• India deployed a new contingent of peacekeepers to a United Nations Peacekeeping Mission in a conflict-affected region.
• The deployment reaffirms India's long-standing commitment to global peace, security, and multilateralism.
• The contingent comprises specialized personnel, including women peacekeepers, to contribute to stability operations, humanitarian aid, and capacity building.
• India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UN Peacekeeping operations globally.
• The move underscores India's active role in international efforts to resolve conflicts and protect civilians.
gsSubTopics: important international institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India is among the largest contributors of troops to UN Peacekeeping Missions.
UN Peacekeeping operations are guided by three basic principles: consent of the parties, impartiality, and non-use of force except in self-defence and defence of the mandate.
India has contributed over 250,000 troops to 49 UN Peacekeeping Missions since 1948.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's historical and contemporary role in UN Peacekeeping operations and its significance for India's foreign policy.
Discuss the challenges faced by UN peacekeepers in conflict zones and the contributions of Indian contingents.
Examine the evolving nature of peacekeeping missions and the need for specialized capabilities, including women peacekeepers.
Evaluate how India's participation in UN Peacekeeping aligns with its aspirations for a greater role in global governance.
articles.imageDescription: Indian UN peacekeepers in blue helmets on a mission in a foreign country.
articles.trivia: India was the first country to deploy an all-women contingent to a UN Peacekeeping mission in Liberia in 2007.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Driving Unprecedented Financial Inclusion, Global Recognition Grows
articles.explainer:
• India's robust Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), including Aadhaar, UPI, and Jan Dhan accounts, continues to drive unprecedented financial inclusion.
• New reports highlight how DPI has enabled millions of unbanked individuals to access formal financial services, credit, and government benefits.
• The success of India's DPI model is gaining global recognition, with several developing nations seeking to replicate similar frameworks.
• Discussions are ongoing about further leveraging DPI for micro-lending, insurance, and digital literacy initiatives.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access, cyber security, and data protection for all users.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
inclusive growth and issues arising from it
science and technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar provides a unique 12-digit identification number.
UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) aims to provide universal access to banking facilities.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private service delivery.
mainsPoints: Explain the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its components in the Indian context.
Analyze how India's DPI model has significantly contributed to financial inclusion and economic development.
Discuss the opportunities and challenges in leveraging DPI for further socio-economic transformation, including micro-lending and digital literacy.
Examine the lessons other developing countries can draw from India's experience in building and scaling DPI.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and Jan Dhan accounts.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 100 billion transactions in 2023, making it the world's leading real-time payment system.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Health Ministerial, Emphasizes Global Health Architecture and Pandemic Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the G20 Health Ministerial Meeting, bringing together health ministers from leading economies.
• The discussions focused on strengthening global health architecture, enhancing pandemic preparedness, and equitable access to vaccines and therapeutics.
• India advocated for a 'One Health' approach, integrating human, animal, and environmental health.
• The meeting highlighted the need for increased investment in public health infrastructure and research & development.
• A joint declaration was adopted, outlining commitments to collaborative action on future health crises.
gsSubTopics: important international institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: G20 includes 19 countries and the European Union, representing major global economies.
The 'One Health' concept recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
India played a significant role in vaccine development and distribution during the COVID-19 pandemic.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in shaping global health policy within multilateral forums like the G20.
Analyze the key challenges in strengthening global health architecture and enhancing pandemic preparedness post-COVID-19.
Explain the 'One Health' approach and its relevance in addressing emerging infectious diseases and antimicrobial resistance.
Propose strategies for ensuring equitable access to health technologies and strengthening public health systems globally.
articles.imageDescription: Health ministers from G20 nations engaged in discussions at a conference table.
articles.trivia: India launched the 'Ayushman Bharat' program, the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

articles.headline: India Semiconductor Mission Accelerates Domestic Chip Manufacturing, Attracts Key Players
articles.explainer:
• The India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) reported significant progress in attracting investment and fostering domestic chip manufacturing capabilities.
• Several global semiconductor giants and domestic conglomerates have announced plans for setting up fabrication units and design centers.
• Policy incentives, including production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes, are driving this momentum.
• The mission aims to establish a resilient semiconductor supply chain, crucial for India's digital economy and strategic autonomy.
• Challenges include skilled workforce development and access to advanced technology.
gsSubTopics: science and technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
investment models
prelimsPoints: India Semiconductor Mission was launched in 2021 with an outlay of ₹76,000 crore.
Semiconductors are essential components in almost all electronic devices.
PLI schemes are designed to boost domestic manufacturing and exports.
A 'fab' or foundry is a semiconductor fabrication plant.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor ecosystem for India's economic and national security interests.
Analyze the key policy interventions under the India Semiconductor Mission and their effectiveness in attracting investment.
Examine the challenges in establishing advanced semiconductor manufacturing in India, including technological gaps and human capital requirements.
Propose measures to accelerate research and development, skill development, and international collaborations to make India a global hub for semiconductors.
articles.imageDescription: A microchip being handled in a cleanroom environment, symbolizing semiconductor manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is projected to reach over $1 trillion by 2030.

articles.headline: Government Enhances Schemes for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Amidst Changing Weather Patterns
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced enhancements to existing schemes aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture.
• These include increased subsidies for drought-resistant seeds, precision irrigation techniques, and crop diversification programs.
• The move comes in response to increasing frequency of extreme weather events impacting agricultural productivity across India.
• The focus is on empowering farmers with technologies and practices to adapt to climate change and ensure food security.
• Training and capacity-building initiatives for farmers are being scaled up nationwide.
gsSubTopics: conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
cropping patterns in different parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
keyTerms: National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change
prelimsPoints: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture.
Precision irrigation techniques include drip and sprinkler irrigation.
Crop diversification helps reduce risks associated with mono-cropping and climate variability.
Schemes like Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana promote organic farming.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and food security.
Analyze the government's initiatives and schemes aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture and their effectiveness.
Examine the role of technology, research, and farmer education in building adaptive capacities in the agricultural sector.
Propose a holistic strategy for sustainable and climate-smart agriculture that integrates traditional wisdom with modern science.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting drought-resistant crops in a field under a changing sky.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top producers of several agricultural commodities, including rice, wheat, and pulses.

highlights: J&K witnesses police raids on bookstores, seizing 668 books linked to outlawed Jamaat-e-Islami, sparking debate on free speech vs. security.
Tragic road accident claims 10 lives of pilgrims en route to Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, raising critical questions on pilgrim safety.
Crowd crush at New Delhi railway station results in 18 fatalities, prompting an urgent review of public safety and disaster management protocols.
RBI maintains status quo on key policy rates amidst volatile inflation, signaling a cautious approach to economic stability.
India's National Green Hydrogen Mission gains momentum with new industry investments and policy incentives, aiming for global leadership.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India is/are correct?
1. Article 44 of the Indian Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy calling for a UCC.
2. Goa is the only Indian state currently having a Uniform Civil Code.
3. The UCC aims to replace all personal laws based on religious scriptures with a common set of laws for all citizens.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Article 44 is part of the DPSP and mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC. Statement 2 is correct: Goa is the only Indian state with a UCC, inherited from its Portuguese civil code. Statement 3 is correct: The primary aim of the UCC is to provide a single law governing matters like marriage, divorce, adoption, inheritance, etc., for all citizens, irrespective of their religion.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM):
1. It aims to make India a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing and design.
2. Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are a part of ISM to attract investments.
3. The mission exclusively focuses on manufacturing advanced logic chips.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The ISM's primary goal is to establish India as a hub for semiconductors. Statement 2 is correct: PLI schemes are a key component of ISM to incentivize domestic and global players. Statement 3 is incorrect: While advanced logic chips are a focus, ISM also aims to support other areas like display fabs, compound semiconductors, packaging, and design, not exclusively advanced logic chips.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Mars Orbiter Mission-2 (MOM-2), consider the following statements:
1. MOM-2 is ISRO's second interplanetary mission to Mars.
2. It aims for a more ambitious scientific exploration, potentially including a lander or rover.
3. India was the first Asian nation to successfully reach Mars orbit with MOM-1.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: MOM-2 is indeed ISRO's second mission to Mars, following the successful MOM-1. Statement 2 is correct: As per the news, MOM-2 is designed for more advanced scientific exploration, including the possibility of a lander/rover. Statement 3 is correct: India, through MOM-1, achieved the distinction of being the first Asian nation to successfully reach Mars orbit.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary objective of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (a) To manage government expenditure and revenue.
(b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
(c) To regulate foreign exchange reserves and exchange rates.
(d) To promote financial literacy and consumer protection.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), as mandated by the RBI Act, is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth. This involves setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to control inflation and support economic expansion.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'One Health' approach, advocated by India at the G20 Health Ministerial, primarily emphasizes:
options: (a) Universal healthcare access for all citizens globally.
(b) Integrated human, animal, and environmental health for addressing global health challenges.
(c) A single global health organization to coordinate pandemic responses.
(d) Prioritizing mental health over physical health in public health policies.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Integrated human, animal, and environmental health for addressing global health challenges.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'One Health' approach recognizes that the health of humans, animals, and the environment are interconnected. It emphasizes a collaborative, multisectoral, and transdisciplinary approach to address health threats like zoonotic diseases, food safety, antimicrobial resistance, and environmental contamination.

mainsMocks.question: The recent police raids in Jammu and Kashmir, targeting publications linked to an outlawed organization, highlight a complex interplay between national security and fundamental rights. Discuss the constitutional provisions governing freedom of speech and expression, and critically analyze the challenges in balancing these rights with internal security imperatives in sensitive regions like J&K.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights; GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the J&K raids and the inherent tension. Discuss Article 19(1)(a) and 19(2) (reasonable restrictions). Analyze the arguments for state action (national security, curbing radicalization) and counter-arguments (freedom of press, academic freedom, potential for misuse). Use specific examples relevant to J&K. Conclude with measures for striking a balance, ensuring transparency, and upholding rule of law.

mainsMocks.question: Large-scale public gatherings, such as the Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela or railway stations, often pose significant crowd management and disaster prevention challenges. In light of the recent tragedies in Prayagraj and New Delhi, critically evaluate India's existing disaster management framework for such events and suggest comprehensive strategies to enhance public safety and emergency response mechanisms.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Social problems, disaster and disaster management; GS3: Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by acknowledging the recent incidents and their human cost. Briefly explain India's disaster management structure (NDMA, SDMA). Analyze the specific challenges in crowd management (overcrowding, inadequate infrastructure, lack of coordination). Suggest strategies: advanced crowd monitoring technologies, improved infrastructure design, robust emergency drills, inter-agency coordination, public awareness campaigns, and legal accountability.

mainsMocks.question: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) has been a game-changer for financial inclusion and efficient service delivery. Examine the key components of India's DPI and their role in promoting inclusive growth. What are the potential challenges and ethical considerations associated with the widespread adoption of DPI, and how can they be addressed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; inclusive growth and issues arising from it; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and identify its key components (Aadhaar, UPI, Jan Dhan, DigiLocker). Explain how each component contributes to financial inclusion, direct benefit transfers, and ease of doing business. Discuss challenges: digital divide, data privacy and security, algorithmic bias, exclusion errors, and potential for surveillance. Suggest solutions: robust data protection laws, digital literacy, grievance redressal mechanisms, and ethical AI frameworks.

quickBytes.text: Jamaat-e-Islami, linked to the seized books in J&K, is an Islamic socio-religious and political organization.
The Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela is held at the confluence of Ganga, Yamuna, and mythical Saraswati rivers.
Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees the right to freedom of speech and expression.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members, with the RBI Governor as ex-officio chairperson.
India and UAE signed the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) in February 2022.
PM-GatiShakti is a national master plan for multi-modal connectivity, integrating 16 ministries.
The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
The Western Ghats are a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide functional household tap connections to all rural households by 2024.

GS1.headline: Tragic Collision Claims Ten Lives En Route to Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, Raises Pilgrim Safety Concerns
GS1.explainer:
• An SUV carrying pilgrims collided with a bus in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh.
• All ten occupants of the SUV, who were en route to the 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, were killed.
• The accident occurred on a busy highway, prompting an immediate investigation into its causes.
• The incident has highlighted critical issues regarding road safety and pilgrim transportation during major religious gatherings.
• Authorities have announced ex-gratia payments and initiated measures to review safety protocols for the ongoing Mela.
gsSubTopics: social problems
disaster and disaster management
role of voluntary organizations
prelimsPoints: Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela is one of the largest religious gatherings globally.
Kumbh Mela is held every 12 years at four different locations: Prayagraj, Haridwar, Ujjain, and Nashik.
National Road Safety Policy aims to reduce road accident fatalities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges in ensuring road safety and crowd management during large-scale religious events like the Kumbh Mela.
Discuss the role of technology and inter-agency coordination in preventing such accidents.
Propose comprehensive strategies for pilgrim safety, including vehicle fitness, driver training, and emergency response.
Evaluate the economic and social impact of such accidents on affected communities and the tourism sector.
GS1.imageDescription: Emergency services at the site of the tragic vehicle collision in Prayagraj.
GS1.trivia: The Kumbh Mela was inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2017.

GS1.headline: Crowd Crush at New Delhi Railway Station Kills 18, Prompts Urgent Review of Public Safety Protocols
GS1.explainer:
• At least 18 people were killed in a crowd crush incident at New Delhi railway station.
• The incident occurred during peak hours, reportedly due to overcrowding on a specific platform and inadequate crowd control measures.
• Emergency services were swiftly deployed, but the high density of people made rescue operations challenging.
• The tragedy has led to a major public outcry and calls for a thorough investigation into railway station management and disaster preparedness.
• The Ministry of Railways has ordered a high-level inquiry and announced compensation for the victims' families.
gsSubTopics: urbanization
their problems and their remedies
disaster and disaster management
public infrastructure
prelimsPoints: New Delhi Railway Station is one of India's busiest railway hubs.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) provides guidelines for crowd management.
Indian Railways operates under the Ministry of Railways.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to crowd crush incidents in public spaces, particularly in densely populated urban centers.
Evaluate the existing public safety and disaster management protocols in Indian railway stations and suggest improvements.
Analyze the role of urban planning, infrastructure development, and technology in preventing such tragedies.
Examine the accountability mechanisms for public authorities in ensuring the safety of commuters.
GS1.imageDescription: Emergency personnel attending to victims at the New Delhi railway station after a crowd crush.
GS1.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world, carrying over 8 billion passengers annually.

GS2.headline: Police Raids in J&K Target Books Linked to Outlawed Jamaat-e-Islami, Raise Free Speech Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• A series of police raids were conducted across bookstores in Jammu and Kashmir.
• 668 books published by an entity associated with the outlawed Islamic organisation Jamaat-e-Islami were seized.
• Authorities stated the action was taken to curb the spread of radical ideology and maintain public order.
• Civil rights groups and some political analysts have raised concerns about freedom of expression and academic liberty.
• The incident highlights ongoing tensions regarding security and fundamental rights in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights
freedom of speech and expression
security challenges and their management in border areas
role of state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: Jamaat-e-Islami is an outlawed organization in India.
Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
Reasonable restrictions on freedom of speech are outlined in Article 19(2).
Jammu and Kashmir's status post-Article 370 abrogation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the balance between national security and fundamental rights, specifically freedom of expression, in sensitive regions like J&K.
Analyze the implications of banning publications for intellectual discourse and the spread of ideologies.
Examine the legal framework (e.g., UAPA) used to justify such actions and its potential for misuse.
Suggest measures to counter radicalization while upholding constitutional liberties.
GS2.imageDescription: Police officials inspecting seized books in a bookstore in Jammu and Kashmir.
GS2.trivia: The first major crackdown on Jamaat-e-Islami in J&K occurred in the late 1980s, leading to its initial ban.

GS2.headline: India and UAE Hold High-Level Review of CEPA, Eyeing Enhanced Trade and Investment
GS2.explainer:
• India and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) held a joint committee meeting to review the progress of their Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA).
• The review focused on identifying new avenues for cooperation and addressing implementation challenges.
• Both nations expressed satisfaction with the significant boost in bilateral trade and investment since CEPA's implementation.
• Discussions included expanding cooperation in green energy, digital trade, and logistics.
• The CEPA aims to increase bilateral non-oil trade to $100 billion by 2030.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: CEPA between India and UAE came into effect in May 2022.
UAE is India's third-largest trading partner and second-largest export destination.
CEPA covers trade in goods, services, rules of origin, customs procedures, and digital trade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-UAE CEPA for both nations.
Discuss the impact of CEPA on India's trade balance, export diversification, and energy security.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in expanding bilateral cooperation beyond traditional trade areas, such as green energy and digital economy.
Examine how such bilateral agreements contribute to India's broader foreign policy objectives and economic diplomacy.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and UAE flags displayed during a high-level bilateral meeting.
GS2.trivia: UAE is home to over 3.5 million Indian expatriates, forming the largest expatriate community in the country.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Aadhaar Linkage for Select Welfare Schemes, Stresses Data Protection
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment regarding the mandatory linkage of Aadhaar for certain government welfare schemes.
• The court largely upheld the constitutional validity of Aadhaar linkage for schemes that involve direct benefit transfers, citing prevention of leakages.
• However, it emphasized the need for robust data protection mechanisms and strict adherence to privacy principles.
• The verdict provides clarity on the balance between state's welfare objectives and citizens' right to privacy.
• The judgment also called for a review of the existing Aadhaar Act to strengthen data security provisions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
right to privacy
prelimsPoints: The Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act was passed in 2016.
The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right under Article 21.
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) scheme aims to transfer subsidies directly to beneficiaries' bank accounts.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's rationale in balancing the state's interest in efficient welfare delivery with the individual's right to privacy concerning Aadhaar.
Discuss the implications of this judgment for the implementation of government welfare schemes and the future of India's digital public infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in ensuring data security and preventing misuse of Aadhaar data, particularly in the absence of a comprehensive data protection law.
Propose legislative and technological measures to strengthen privacy safeguards in the context of digital identity systems.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The Aadhaar project was initially conceived in 2009 under the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI).

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Global Food Security at G20 Ministerial Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India played a prominent role at the G20 Agriculture Ministers' Meeting, advocating for a more equitable and resilient global food security system.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for sustainable agricultural practices, diversification of food sources, and reduction of food waste.
• Discussions included addressing the impact of climate change on agriculture and ensuring fair access to food for vulnerable populations.
• India highlighted its own initiatives, such as the National Food Security Act and promotion of millets, as models for other nations.
• The meeting concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing cooperation to tackle global hunger and malnutrition.
gsSubTopics: important international institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
issues relating to poverty and hunger
effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The National Food Security Act (NFSA) 2013 aims to provide subsidized food grains to approximately two-thirds of India's population.
The year 2023 was declared as the International Year of Millets by the UN, at India's initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and contributions to global food security initiatives within multilateral forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges to achieving global food security, including climate change, geopolitical conflicts, and supply chain disruptions.
Evaluate India's domestic policies and programs aimed at ensuring food security and their potential for replication elsewhere.
Propose strategies for international cooperation to build resilient and sustainable food systems globally.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from G20 nations attending a ministerial meeting on agriculture and food security.
GS2.trivia: India holds 11.5% of the world's total livestock population, making it a significant player in global food production.

GS2.headline: Public Consultations Continue on Uniform Civil Code, Highlighting Diverse Views and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• Nationwide public consultations on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) are ongoing, with various religious and civil society groups submitting their views.
• The Law Commission of India is actively engaging with stakeholders to understand the complexities and implications of a UCC.
• Discussions reveal a wide spectrum of opinions, ranging from strong support for gender justice and national integration to concerns about religious freedom and cultural diversity.
• The process aims to build consensus and address apprehensions regarding the potential impact on personal laws of different communities.
• The government reiterates its commitment to a comprehensive and inclusive approach before any legislative proposal.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution states that the State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
Personal laws in India are currently governed by religious scriptures and customs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and historical background of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, national integration, and religious freedom.
Examine the challenges in achieving consensus on UCC, given India's diverse cultural and religious landscape.
Propose a roadmap for a phased and inclusive approach to UCC implementation, addressing concerns of various communities.
GS2.imageDescription: A conceptual image depicting diverse religious symbols converging, representing the Uniform Civil Code debate.
GS2.trivia: The first Law Commission in independent India was established in 1955.

GS2.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Providing Tap Water to Rural Households
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), highlighting significant achievements in rural tap water connectivity.
• The mission has successfully provided functional household tap connections (FHTC) to millions of additional rural households across the country.
• Several states and Union Territories are nearing 100% saturation, demonstrating effective implementation and community participation.
• The focus remains on ensuring long-term sustainability of water sources and water quality monitoring.
• JJM aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections by 2024.
gsSubTopics: government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission was launched in August 2019.
It comes under the Ministry of Jal Shakti.
The mission aims to provide FHTC to every rural household by 2024.
Pani Samitis (Village Water and Sanitation Committees) play a crucial role in local implementation.
mainsPoints: Assess the impact of the Jal Jeevan Mission on rural livelihoods, health, and women's empowerment.
Discuss the key strategies and challenges in achieving 100% tap water connectivity in diverse geographical regions of India.
Examine the role of community participation and decentralized governance (Pani Samitis) in the success of JJM.
Propose measures to ensure the long-term sustainability of water sources and address issues of water quality and equitable distribution.
GS2.imageDescription: A rural woman collecting water from a newly installed tap connection.
GS2.trivia: The 'Har Ghar Jal' (Tap water to every home) initiative is a major component of the Jal Jeevan Mission.

GS2.headline: India Deploys New Contingent to UN Peacekeeping Mission, Reaffirms Commitment to Global Peace
GS2.explainer:
• India deployed a new contingent of peacekeepers to a United Nations Peacekeeping Mission in a conflict-affected region.
• The deployment reaffirms India's long-standing commitment to global peace, security, and multilateralism.
• The contingent comprises specialized personnel, including women peacekeepers, to contribute to stability operations, humanitarian aid, and capacity building.
• India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UN Peacekeeping operations globally.
• The move underscores India's active role in international efforts to resolve conflicts and protect civilians.
gsSubTopics: important international institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
India and its neighborhood- relations
bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India is among the largest contributors of troops to UN Peacekeeping Missions.
UN Peacekeeping operations are guided by three basic principles: consent of the parties, impartiality, and non-use of force except in self-defence and defence of the mandate.
India has contributed over 250,000 troops to 49 UN Peacekeeping Missions since 1948.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's historical and contemporary role in UN Peacekeeping operations and its significance for India's foreign policy.
Discuss the challenges faced by UN peacekeepers in conflict zones and the contributions of Indian contingents.
Examine the evolving nature of peacekeeping missions and the need for specialized capabilities, including women peacekeepers.
Evaluate how India's participation in UN Peacekeeping aligns with its aspirations for a greater role in global governance.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian UN peacekeepers in blue helmets on a mission in a foreign country.
GS2.trivia: India was the first country to deploy an all-women contingent to a UN Peacekeeping mission in Liberia in 2007.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Health Ministerial, Emphasizes Global Health Architecture and Pandemic Preparedness
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the G20 Health Ministerial Meeting, bringing together health ministers from leading economies.
• The discussions focused on strengthening global health architecture, enhancing pandemic preparedness, and equitable access to vaccines and therapeutics.
• India advocated for a 'One Health' approach, integrating human, animal, and environmental health.
• The meeting highlighted the need for increased investment in public health infrastructure and research & development.
• A joint declaration was adopted, outlining commitments to collaborative action on future health crises.
gsSubTopics: important international institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: G20 includes 19 countries and the European Union, representing major global economies.
The 'One Health' concept recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
India played a significant role in vaccine development and distribution during the COVID-19 pandemic.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in shaping global health policy within multilateral forums like the G20.
Analyze the key challenges in strengthening global health architecture and enhancing pandemic preparedness post-COVID-19.
Explain the 'One Health' approach and its relevance in addressing emerging infectious diseases and antimicrobial resistance.
Propose strategies for ensuring equitable access to health technologies and strengthening public health systems globally.
GS2.imageDescription: Health ministers from G20 nations engaged in discussions at a conference table.
GS2.trivia: India launched the 'Ayushman Bharat' program, the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Volatile Inflation, Signals Cautious Optimism
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest review meeting.
• The MPC decided to maintain the repo rate and reverse repo rate at existing levels, opting for a status quo.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of persistently volatile retail inflation and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted concerns over food inflation but expressed cautious optimism regarding future growth prospects.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the RBI, with potential for future adjustments based on evolving economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
inflation
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain a status quo on policy rates.
Discuss the challenges posed by food inflation and global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy framework.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in achieving both price stability and economic growth.
Examine the potential impact of the RBI's stance on various sectors of the economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The first meeting of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was held in October 2016.

GS3.headline: PM-GatiShakti's Second Phase Focuses on Integrated Urban Infrastructure Development
GS3.explainer:
• The government announced the initiation of the second phase of the PM-GatiShakti National Master Plan, specifically targeting urban infrastructure.
• This phase aims to integrate planning and execution of projects like metro extensions, smart city initiatives, and urban logistics hubs.
• The initiative leverages geospatial technology and data analytics for efficient resource allocation and project monitoring.
• The goal is to enhance urban connectivity, reduce logistics costs, and improve the quality of life in Indian cities.
• Stakeholders from various ministries and state governments are being brought together for coordinated implementation.
gsSubTopics: infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
investment models
planning
mobilization of resources
prelimsPoints: PM-GatiShakti was launched in October 2021.
It is a digital platform to bring 16 ministries together for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
The core principle is 'holistic planning' rather than 'siloed planning'.
It uses a GIS-based platform for visualization and planning.
mainsPoints: Explain how the PM-GatiShakti framework can revolutionize urban infrastructure development in India.
Discuss the benefits of integrated planning and execution of projects for urban logistics and economic efficiency.
Analyze the challenges in implementing such large-scale multi-sectoral projects, particularly concerning inter-ministerial coordination and land acquisition.
Suggest measures to ensure inclusive and sustainable urban development under PM-GatiShakti.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital map showing integrated urban infrastructure projects under PM-GatiShakti.
GS3.trivia: The PM-GatiShakti portal integrates satellite imagery and real-time data to track project progress.

GS3.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Mars Orbiter Mission-2 (MOM-2) Development, Aims for Advanced Martian Science
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) released an update on the progress of its Mars Orbiter Mission-2 (MOM-2), also known as Mangalyaan-2.
• Scientists confirmed that significant advancements have been made in payload development and mission architecture.
• MOM-2 aims for a more ambitious scientific exploration of Mars, focusing on surface features, atmospheric dynamics, and potential biosignatures.
• The mission will incorporate advanced instruments and potentially a lander or rover component for in-situ studies.
• The launch is tentatively scheduled for the late 2020s, building upon the success of the first Mars Orbiter Mission.
gsSubTopics: achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
space technology
prelimsPoints: India's first Mars Orbiter Mission (MOM) was launched in 2013 and successfully inserted into Mars orbit in 2014.
MOM made India the first Asian nation and ISRO the fourth space agency to reach Mars orbit.
MOM-2 is envisioned as a follow-up mission with more advanced scientific objectives.
PSLV and GSLV are ISRO's primary launch vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's Mars exploration program for its scientific capabilities and global standing in space technology.
Analyze the scientific objectives of MOM-2 and how they differ from or build upon MOM-1.
Examine the technological challenges involved in interplanetary missions, particularly for a lander/rover component.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits and spin-off technologies derived from space exploration missions for India.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Mars Orbiter Mission-2 spacecraft orbiting Mars.
GS3.trivia: MOM-1 was the cheapest Mars mission to date, costing approximately $74 million.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Gathers Momentum with New Industry Investments and Policy Incentives
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is showing significant progress, attracting substantial investments from both domestic and international players.
• The government announced new policy incentives and subsidies to accelerate the development of green hydrogen production and infrastructure.
• Several major industrial conglomerates have unveiled plans for large-scale green hydrogen projects, including electrolyzer manufacturing.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Challenges remain in scaling up production, reducing costs, and developing a robust supply chain.
gsSubTopics: conservation
environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
science and technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission was approved by the Union Cabinet in January 2023.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
It aims to reduce reliance on fossil fuels and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
Electrolyzers are key equipment for green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Explain the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security, climate goals, and economic growth.
Discuss the key pillars and policy incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Analyze the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Suggest measures to foster a robust ecosystem for research, manufacturing, and deployment of green hydrogen technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: An industrial facility depicting green hydrogen production via electrolysis powered by wind turbines.
GS3.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Draft Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence Governance, Emphasizing Ethical Use
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released a draft policy framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance in India.
• The framework focuses on promoting responsible AI development, ensuring ethical deployment, and mitigating potential risks.
• Key areas addressed include data privacy, algorithmic bias, accountability, and the socio-economic impact of AI.
• The government aims to foster innovation while establishing guardrails to prevent misuse and ensure public trust in AI technologies.
• Public consultations have been invited to gather feedback from stakeholders across industry, academia, and civil society.
